topic: help with a reel showing her morning market run

turns.content: hiii okay I need your creative brain na ka!! I want to make an Instagram reel about my morning market run. like the FULL experience — 4am alarm, dark sky, the market, everything. I think my followers will love it because everyone always ask me 'Pan how do you wake up so early??' and I want to SHOW them haha

That's a great idea! Behind-the-scenes content always performs well, and your 4am routine is the kind of thing that makes people's jaws drop. Reels that show the hustle behind the food tend to get a lot of shares too.

Let's plan this out. How long are you thinking — 30 seconds, 60 seconds, or 90? And do you have someone who can film you, or are you doing this solo with a phone tripod?

I think 60 seconds is good na ka. not too long, not too short. my friend can film some parts but at the market I think I just use tripod and my phone because my friend is NOT waking up at 4am for me haha 😂 she say 'Pan I love you but no'

Haha can't blame her! Okay, 60 seconds is perfect for a reel — long enough to tell a story but short enough to hold attention. Here's a rough shot list you could follow:

1. **Opening** (5 sec): Alarm going off at 4am, dark room, you getting up
2. **The ride** (5 sec): Quick clip of the road while it's still dark
3. **Market arrival** (10 sec): Walking into the market — show the lights, the energy, vendors setting up
4. **Shopping** (15 sec): You picking ingredients, inspecting chicken, grabbing vegetables, loading bags
5. **The haul** (10 sec): Everything you bought laid out — the fresh ingredients looking beautiful
6. **Prep begins** (10 sec): Back at your spot, lighting the charcoal, starting the day
7. **Closing** (5 sec): First plate served, smile to camera

Does that flow feel right for your routine?

arai wa this is so good!! I love the alarm opening — very dramatic haha. and yes the market has this ENERGY at 4am that people don't know about na ka. all the lights, the vendors yelling, the ice everywhere. it's like a whole world that most people are sleeping through. I definitely need to capture that feeling

Exactly — that hidden world energy is what will hook viewers. Most people have never seen a wholesale market at 4am and it looks incredible on camera with all the fluorescent lights against the dark sky.

A few filming tips for the market:
- Try to get some wide shots showing the scale — rows of stalls, trucks unloading, the bustle
- Close-ups of the ingredients — fresh chicken, ice glistening, herbs piled up
- A shot of you chatting with your supplier or handing over cash — that personal connection moment

What song or audio are you thinking? A trending sound could boost your reach.

ooh good point about the music! I was thinking maybe a Thai song but then international followers won't connect na ka. what about one of those trending motivational type beats?? like the ones that go with 'grind culture' content haha. because this IS grind culture, just Thai style 🇹🇭

Thai-style grind culture, I love that! For music you have a few options:

1. **Trending audio** — Check your Reels tab for what's popular right now. Using a trending sound gets you pushed to more people by the algorithm
2. **Motivational beat** — Something with a slow build that picks up energy works perfectly with your dark-to-dawn story arc
3. **Original audio with voiceover** — You narrate in Tinglish over the real market sounds. This is actually the most engaging because YOUR voice is the brand

Honestly, option 3 could be amazing. Imagine the real market sounds — clanking ice, vendors shouting, motorbike engines — with you narrating like "4am. everybody sleeping. but my chicken is not going to buy itself." That's gold.

OPTION 3!! yes yes yes!! oh my god the voiceover idea is so good na ka. because the market SOUNDS are part of the experience — the ice crashing, the aunties yelling, the cart wheels. okay wait can you help me write a short voiceover script?? like just a few lines, keep it casual, keep it me

Yes! Here's a voiceover draft — short lines that match each shot:

**[Alarm / dark room]** "4am. Every day. No days off."

**[Riding to market]** "While Bangkok is sleeping... I'm already on the road."

**[Market arrival — wide shot]** "This is where it starts. Before the first plate, before the first customer."

**[Shopping — picking chicken, inspecting quality]** "Best chicken. Best rice. No shortcuts."

**[The haul / loading up]** "20 kilos of rice, 40 kilos of chicken. Every single day."

**[Lighting charcoal / prep]** "By 6am, the charcoal is ready. By 10am, the line starts."

**[First plate / smile]** "350 plates. One recipe. Three generations."

Keep each line short and punchy. Let the visuals and the market sounds do the heavy lifting between your lines.

I am screaming na ka this is PERFECT!! '350 plates. One recipe. Three generations.' that line gave me goosebumps haha. okay but should I do the voiceover in English or Thai?? or mix?? my followers are like 70% Thai and 30% international

With that audience split, I'd actually say do it in your natural Tinglish — the Thai-English mix you already use. It's authentic to you and both audiences will understand the key phrases. The short punchy lines are mostly simple English that Thai speakers also get, and international viewers can follow along with the visuals even if they miss a word.

You could add Thai subtitles AND English subtitles at the bottom. That way everyone can follow along and it looks professional. Instagram even has auto-caption features now that you can edit.

The Tinglish voice IS your brand. Don't lose that for either audience.

Tinglish is my brand haha I love that!! okay true true. and subtitle is smart because then the reel work even on mute na ka. a lot of people scroll with no sound. okay one more question — what time should I POST it?? I always struggle with timing

Good thinking about the mute scrollers — subtitles are essential for that reason.

For posting time, with your 70/30 Thai-international split:
- **Best time for Thai audience**: 12pm-1pm (lunch break scrolling) or 8pm-9pm (evening relaxing)
- **Best time for international reach**: 7pm-9pm Bangkok time catches morning scrollers in Europe and late night in the US

I'd suggest posting around **8pm Bangkok time** — it hits both audiences. Plus there's something poetic about posting a 4am hustle video while everyone is relaxing on their couch at night. Makes the impact hit harder.

Also, post it on a Tuesday or Wednesday — those tend to have the best engagement. Weekends are actually weaker for Instagram reach.

8pm Tuesday or Wednesday, got it na ka!! I am going to film this Monday morning and edit Monday night and post Tuesday 8pm. perfect plan!! thank you so much for helping me with this. I was going to just randomly film stuff at the market but now I have a real plan with a shot list and script and everything haha. you are like my creative director 😂🙏

Haha your creative director who works for free! But seriously, you've got a solid plan now. Monday film, Monday night edit, Tuesday 8pm post — that's professional.

Last tips:
- Film way more than you need — you can always cut but you can't reshoot 4am
- Wipe your camera lens before you start, market humidity fogs it up
- For the voiceover, record it separately in a quiet place after. Layer it over the footage in CapCut or the Instagram editor
- Use 3-5 relevant hashtags, not 30. Something like #BangkokStreetFood #4amHustle #KhaoManGai

This reel is going to be fire. Your followers will have a whole new respect for that plate of chicken rice when they see what goes into it before sunrise. Can't wait to hear how it does! 🔥🙏
